pr.title: Remove extern float declarations to match ShadowAlloc
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Removed unused or counterproductive extern float declarations from src/sysdolphin/baselib/shadow.c and replaced the symbolic float constant used in HSD_ShadowAlloc with literal 256.f arguments, allowing ShadowAlloc to match. The PR was a very small decomp-matching cleanup with no discussion or review comments in the provided slice.
what_changed: Deleted extern const f32 declarations for lbl_804DE768, lbl_804DE76C, HSD_Shadow_804DE770, and HSD_Shadow_804DE774 from shadow.c.
Changed HSD_ShadowAlloc to call HSD_CObjSetViewportfx4(shadow->camera, 0.0f, 256.f, 0.0f, 256.f) directly instead of passing HSD_Shadow_804DE774 for the two 256-valued float arguments.
smart_moves: Preferred direct float literals where the generated code needed them, rather than preserving artificial extern float labels that prevented the function from matching.
Kept the change scoped to the exact matching issue in HSD_ShadowAlloc, avoiding broader refactors in shadow.c.
Removed now-unneeded float label declarations once the only shown use of HSD_Shadow_804DE774 was replaced by 256.f.
decomp_lessons: For matching work, not every rodata-looking float label should be modeled as an extern symbol; sometimes the compiler emits the desired code only when the source uses an immediate float literal.
If a named float constant exists only to satisfy an earlier nonmatching hypothesis, re-test whether a literal produces better code before keeping the extern declaration.
Small cleanup PRs can be valuable when they remove artificial symbols and make the decompiled source closer to plausible original C.
naming_conventions: Real function and type names in this area use HSD_Shadow and HSD_CObj prefixes, such as HSD_ShadowAlloc, HSD_ShadowGetAllocData, HSD_CObjSetViewportfx4, and HSD_CObjSetScissorx4.

Auto-generated or placeholder float labels used address-like names such as lbl_804DE768 and HSD_Shadow_804DE774; this PR removed several of those declarations instead of preserving them as source-level names.
The object allocation global remained named shadow_alloc_data with type HSD_ObjAllocData.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use literal 256.f for viewport float arguments when matching HSD_ShadowAlloc rather than an extern const f32 symbol.
Remove unused extern float declarations after replacing artificial constant references, since their presence can indicate stale matching scaffolding.
Keep integer constants as integers for scissor setup while using float constants for viewport setup, matching the called APIs' expected argument types.
searchable_terms: Remove extern float declarations to match ShadowAlloc
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining extern const f32 declarations in sysdolphin baselib files that may be removable in favor of literals.
Check whether lbl_804DE768, lbl_804DE76C, HSD_Shadow_804DE770, or HSD_Shadow_804DE774 remain referenced elsewhere after this PR.
Compare other viewport/scissor setup functions for similar artificial rodata float constants that block matching.
